text: Novice runners have a weighted injury incidence of 17.8 injuries per 1000 hours of running.
Recreational runners have a weighted injury incidence of 7.7 injuries per 1000 hours of running.
Novice runners face approximately 2.3 times greater injury risk per 1000 hours of running compared to recreational runners.
Running-related injury incidence ranges from 2.5 to 33.0 per 1000 hours depending on runner type, with novice runners at the highest end.
population: novice runners, recreational runners, ultramarathon runners, track and field athletes

text: Long-distance track and field athletes have the lowest reported injury incidence at 2.5 per 1000 hours of running.
Beginning runners should be coached with particular attention to injury prevention given their substantially elevated injury risk compared to experienced runners.
